content: Hey, I'm so excited to finally have some time off from my opera performances. I've been touring internationally for months, and I'm looking forward to spending some quality time with my new golden retriever, Willow.
That sounds wonderful! Congratulations on adopting Willow. What's it like having a new furry companion?
It's been amazing. I've even started taking her on mushroom foraging trips in the woods near my home in Vermont. She loves running around and exploring.
Mushroom foraging sounds like a fascinating hobby. Have you always been interested in it, or is it something you've recently taken up?

I've been interested in it for years, but I never had the time to really get into it. Now that I have Willow, it's a great way for us to spend time together. I also love cooking Thai food, and I've been experimenting with using the mushrooms I find in my recipes.
That sounds delicious. Do you have a favorite Thai dish that you like to make with your foraged mushrooms?
Definitely. I love making a good Tom Yum soup with the chanterelles I find. It's such a great way to warm up after a long day of performing.
I'll have to try that recipe out. So, what's next for you in terms of performances? Do you have any upcoming shows or tours planned?
Actually, I have a few shows lined up in Europe next month. I'll be performing in Paris on the 15th and then in Rome on the 22nd. I'm really looking forward to it.
That sounds exciting. Will you have any time to explore the cities while you're there, or will you be pretty busy with rehearsals and performances?
I should have a bit of free time, especially in Paris. I'm planning to visit the Louvre and do some calligraphy in my journal while I'm there. I love practicing my handwriting in different locations.
The Louvre is definitely a must-see. And it's great that you're making time for your hobbies, even while you're touring. Do you find that calligraphy helps you relax?
Yes, it does. I also love doing crossword puzzles in my downtime. It's a great way to keep my mind sharp and relaxed at the same time.
I can see how that would be helpful. So, what do you think is the most challenging part of being an opera singer, and how do you balance your work and personal life?

For me, the most challenging part is probably the travel schedule. It can be tough to maintain a routine and stay connected with loved ones while I'm on the road. But I'm lucky to have a great support system, including my partner, Alex, who is also a musician.
It's great that you have a supportive partner. And finally, what advice would you give to someone who is just starting out in the opera world?

I would say that it's essential to be persistent and keep working on your craft. Take care of your voice, and don't be afraid to try new things and take risks. And always make time for the things that bring you joy, like gardening and playing chess!
